title: Something is eating your server
problem: Your server is sluggish. Someone on the team accidentally left a script running in the background and it is hammering the CPU. Find the process, investigate it, and kill it cleanly. If it does not die, force it.

setup.description: Spawn fake CPU-hungry background processes to simulate the problem.
setup.note: These will spike your CPU. They are harmless and gone the moment you kill them or close the terminal.

hints: top or htop will show you what is eating CPU in real time — sort by CPU column and look for the culprit
Once you spot it, use ps aux | grep yes to find all instances and get their PIDs
kill PID sends a graceful signal — if the process ignores it, kill -9 PID forces it. Kill all instances with pkill yes
solution:
1. See what is running:   top  or  htop  # real time process viewer
2. Sort by CPU:           press P inside top  # P sorts by CPU, M sorts by memory
3. Find all instances:    ps aux | grep yes  # aux shows all processes, grep filters by name
4. Kill one by PID:       kill <PID>  # sends SIGTERM, asks process to stop gracefully
5. Kill all at once:      pkill yes  # kills all processes matching the name
6. Force kill if needed:  kill -9 <PID>  # SIGKILL, cannot be ignored by the process
7. Confirm they are gone: ps aux | grep yes  # should return nothing or just the grep itself
